reasoning: Sentence d shows voiced stops becoming unvoiced at the end of words: need -> neet (d->t), bad -> bat (d->t), and -> ant (d->t), lab -> lap (b->p). This matches rule 1: [+Stop, +Voiced] -> [+Stop, -Voiced] / _ #. Other sentences do not consistently show this pattern.

Sentence c shows double consonants becoming single: really -> realy (ll->l), carrots -> carots (rr->r), trillion -> trilion (ll->l), dollars -> dolars (ll->l), massive -> masive (ss->s), quadrillion -> quatrilion (ll->l). This matches rule 2: [+Cons][+Cons] -> [+Cons]. Other changes like beleive are minor and not the primary rule.

Sentence e shows s becoming z at the beginning of words: smart -> zmart, suit -> zuit, sigh -> Zigh. This matches rule 3: s -> z / # _. Other changes (e.g., spilled -> spiled, synthetic -> sinthetic) are secondary spelling variations not explained by this rule, but the z changes are diagnostic.

Sentence a shows vowels following another vowel being deleted: really (ea -> e, relly), soaked (oa -> o, soked 'so ked'), journey (ou -> o, jorney), floating (oa -> o, floting), oasis (oa -> o, osis). This matches rule 4: [+Vow] -> Ø / [+Vow] _. The spacing in 'so ked' likely represents 'soked'.

Sentence b shows e becoming a before a consonant: Eggplant (e -> a, Aggplant), fuel (e -> a, fual), energy (e -> a twice, anargy), better (e -> a, batter), excellent (e -> a in 'ce', exallant approx. exellant), electric (e -> a, allectric approx.), socket (e -> a, soket 'so ket'). This matches rule 5: e -> a / _ [+Cons]. Some spellings are approximate but the pattern holds; trully is irregular.

Language A is Portuguese, as it shows more advanced changes from Western Romance (e.g., intervocalic c > g as in fogo/fregare, vowel shifts like u > o final shared with Italian but further nasalization with ~ and ^, devoicing t > d in estado, loss of initial i with e insertion in espada). The tree structure places A further from Western Romance with additional rules.

Language B is Italian, closer to Western Romance than A, sharing final u > o (cavallo, stato) and loss of initial i (stato, spata) with Portuguese but retaining gemination (double consonants like cavallo, rotto, secco, castello, labbro, mettere, pollo) and developing uo diphthongs (fuoco, buono, suonare) without Portuguese's further voicing or nasal changes.

Language C branches early from Vulgar Latin, being the most conservative (retains final u as in focu, rutu, cabaddu, istatu, pira, puddu; keeps initial i as in istatu, ispat a; v > b as in abere, cabaddu; fewer shared innovations with Pt/It like no final u > o or initial i loss). Matches Sardinian's real linguistic position as divergent.

From Latin BACULUM to Vulgar *vaculu (intervocalic b > v as in cab allum > cavallu, habere > avere; final m > Ø as in bonum > bonu). Then to Western Romance: final u > o (shared Pt/It innovation as in cavallu > cavalo/cavallo), yielding vaculo. No other shared changes apply (no initial i, d not intervocalic here).

Latin VITA has no h, final m, or intervocalic b, so unchanged to Vulgar vita. Western Romance changes do not apply (no final u, no initial i, no relevant stops or vowels to shift), yielding vita (cf. similar unchanged forms like arena > arena in Pt/It).

Assuming FINDERE (to split; 'FIND ARE' likely transcription for findere). Latin findere has no h, final m, or intervocalic b, so unchanged to Vulgar findere. Western Romance changes do not apply (final e retained as in sonare > sonare/sônare/suonare; intervocalic d remains as no devoicing/voicing trigger in data; no initial i or final u), yielding findere.
